---
title: "How to use this book"
description: "Reading routes, notation, evidence, and practical expectations."
page-role: frontmatter
---

# How to use this book

The default route follows the sidebar from elements to systems. Readers who
already know basic circuit theory can use the chapter prerequisites in
[`chapters.toml`](chapters.toml) to enter later. Institutional course routes
select from the same chapter IDs without forking the book. The
[reading roadmap](roadmap.qmd) provides shorter reader-facing routes, connects
the practical spine, and explains chapter maturity.

## A chapter has three jobs

First, it should make a model intelligible. Second, it should connect that model
to observable evidence. Third, it should use the evidence to make a design or
diagnostic decision. A chapter is incomplete when it performs only the first.

## Representations

Important examples should connect several of these views:

- physical construction and energy path;
- schematic and named nodes;
- equations and assumptions;
- simulation or executable model;
- measured data and uncertainty;
- software, state, or timing;
- requirements and acceptance tests.

## Notation

SI units are used throughout. Voltage polarity and current reference direction
are defined before equations. A negative result means reality opposes the chosen
reference; it is not automatically an error. Approximation, tolerance, bound,
and uncertainty are written distinctly.

## Evidence

The evidence ladder is:

```text
estimate → calculation → simulation → measurement → repeated test
         → independent reproduction → field evidence → qualified claim
```

Not every question needs the final rung. The consequence of being wrong
determines how much evidence is appropriate.

## Building lectures from the book

A lecture should select a small number of chapter sections, surface one
misconception, work one example, and create one decision or exit check. Slides
provide cues; the book provides durable exposition. See the
[instructor guide](../instructor/README.md).
